response.justification: Sarah Smith has a strong web and full-stack background with meaningful distributed systems exposure, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows approximately 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — C++ is not even listed in the skills section. The role centers on leading a low-level cloud compute platform, while the candidate's entire career trajectory is built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Staff-level titles and workshop facilitation show some leadership qualities, but there is no evidence of technical leadership over infrastructure or cloud platform teams at the required depth.
strengths: Real high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency constraints
Demonstrated DevOps and infrastructure ownership: Kubernetes deployments, CI/CD pipelines, GitLab administration, and AWS CDK
Active technical community contributor through workshops, conference talks, and open-source tooling — signals mentoring capacity
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI, one thesis project) against a hard minimum of 8 years — this alone disqualifies the application
No cloud compute platform or internal infrastructure platform leadership; cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK for a direct-mail pricing service
Primary expertise is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) — a fundamentally different engineering domain from systems-level C++ infrastructure
